title: Add Merge-Conflict Bead Type And Guardrail Template
description: Extend bead typing to recognize `merge-conflict` and add the dedicated agent guardrail template used by merge-created resolution beads, including instructions for conflict resolution and post-rebase test verification.
acceptance_criteria: `merge-conflict` is accepted anywhere bead types are validated or serialized.
A new `templates/agents/merge-conflict.md` template exists and is suitable for both merge-conflict and post-merge test-failure corrective contexts.
The new bead type is available for downstream CLI, list, summary, and TUI rendering without being misclassified as `corrective`.

handoff_summary.completed: - Added `BEAD_TYPES = {"task", "epic", "feature", "merge-conflict"}` constant to `models.py` alongside `AGENT_TYPES` and `MUTATING_AGENTS`. - Created `templates/agents/merge-conflict.md` with full developer-style guardrails adapted for conflict resolution and post-merge test-failure corrective contexts.
handoff_summary.risks: Downstream beads that create merge-conflict beads will need to set `bead_type="merge-conflict"` explicitly — there is no enforcement. The tester bead for this feature should verify BEAD_TYPES is importable and contains `merge-conflict`.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify BEAD_TYPES constant is importable, contains `merge-conflict`, and that the template file exists and is well-formed.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — models.py change is additive (new constant only). Template is a new file with no overlap with other in-progress beads.

conflict_risks: None — models.py change is additive (new constant only). Template is a new file with no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Added `BEAD_TYPES` constant to models.py (includes `merge-conflict`, `task`, `epic`, `feature`) and created `templates/agents/merge-conflict.md` with guardrails covering conflict resolution, post-merge regression fixes, disallowed actions, and required output fields. The new bead type is recognized at the model layer without touching scheduler detection logic — `merge-conflict` IDs will not contain `-corrective` so `_is_corrective_bead` will never misclassify them.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
